questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Saunter
questions.explanation: Saunter – to walk without hurryingStroll – a slow walk for pleasure.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. From this platform he launched his ………… for election to the lower house in 2000.
questions.explanation: Guffaw – a loud and noisy laughterCampaign – a plan series of things to do to achieve a special aim.Impromptu – to do something without preparation.Ambush – a surprise attack from a hidden position.“campaign” is the most appropriate word to be used here.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the right order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. The Nile River was critical to the development of ancient Egypt B. In addition to Egypt, the Nile runs through or along the border of 10 other African countries, namely, Burundi, Tanzania, Rwanda, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, Kenya, Uganda, Sudan, Ethiopia, and South Sudan. Its three main tributaries are the White Nile, the Blue Nile, and the Atbara C. The Nile River flows from south to north through eastern Africa. D. It begins in the rivers that flow into Lake Victoria (located in modern-day Uganda, Tanzania, and Kenya), and empties into the Mediterranean Sea more than 6,600 kilometers (4,100 miles) to the north, making it one of the longest river in the world.
questions.explanation: C is the introductory statement, D is adding some information about the river, Egypt has been brought into the picture by A. “in addition to Egypt” signifies that B should follow A. Hence, the sequence should be CDAB

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. Soft, inner part of a nut or fruit-stone
questions.explanation: Caramel – a sticky sweet made from boiled sugar, butter and milk. Pulp – the soft inner part of some fruits or vegetables

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. whimper
questions.explanation: whimper – to cry softly.Grin – to give a broad smile.Groan – to make a deep sad sound because you are in pain.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. A person who knows a lot about food and cooking.
questions.explanation: Anorexic – one who avoids eating due to fear of gaining weight.Gourmet – A person who knows a lot about food and cooking.Haughty – an arrogant person.Incarnate – (a deity or spirit) in human form.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Sunder
questions.explanation: Sunder – to split apart.

questions.question: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them contains an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. He would/ rather die/ then bag.
questions.explanation: “than bag” instead of “then bag”

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect speech. “I will work hard to get the job,” said Rohan.
options: Rohan said that he could work hard to get the job.
Rohan said that he should work hard to get the job.
Rohan said that he would work hard to get the job.
Rohan said that he ought to work hard to get the job.
questions.explanation: will changes to would when speech is changed from direct to indirect. Only option c has it.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the highlighted segment in the given sentence. If no substitution is required, select ‘No substitution’. There is only one of his books that are interesting.
questions.explanation: “only one” of the books is interesting, so, the verb must be singular.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. ______ your skin with this nourishing night cream.
questions.explanation: Prosper – to grow successful.Pamper – to take care of something or somebody.Percolate – to move gradually through a surface that has very small holes or spaces in it.

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: deity is the correct spelling

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the following idiom. Achilles heels
options: Good massage for aches and pains
a small but very dangerous weakness
questions.explanation: Achilles heel – a small weakness but that can be very dangerous if found by the enemy.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If no substitution is required, select ‘No substitution’. Each of the students/ have to/ work on the project.
questions.explanation: Each of the students should take singular verb because of “each”. So, “has to” instead of “have to”.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. Who wrote the vedas?
options: By who was the vedas written?
Who was that who wrote the vedas?
By whom was that who wrote the vedas?
By whom were the vedas written?
questions.explanation: The given sentence is in the active form.Sub – who object – the vedasIn passive form, the subject and the object interchange their positions. The objective form of who is “by whom”.So, By whom were the vedas written? Is the correct passive form.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Quarantine
questions.explanation: Quarantine – to cut off a person from others to prevent spread of diseaseAssimilate – to allow someone to become a part of a group.Correlate – to show a relationship between two or more thingsImplicate - to show that somebody is involved in a crime.Allude – to speak about somebody in an indirect way

questions.question: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them contains an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. Our maths teacher/ emphasized on/ understanding the formulae.
questions.explanation: emphasized is not followed by on. Emphasized when used as verb is not followed by on.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the following idiom. Castles in the air
options: A safari to spot rare animals
questions.explanation: Castles in the air – to daydream/ an unrealizable hope or desire

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: Camouflage is the right spelling.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the right order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. He was caught by the police on the spot. B. Anil bought a car last week. C. Unfortunately, the car met with an accident today. D. The accident took place due to the carelessness of his driver.
questions.explanation: B is the introductory statement therefore, it must be at the starting. D should follow C as the latter is talking about the accident and the former explaining the same. A is the consequence so, it must be at last.So the correct sequence is BCDA.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each number. Ben committed (1)______ crime when he (2)______ a bank. Someone (3)______ the crime and told the police. The police charged him (4)______ the crime and put him behind bars. They also accused his twin brother, Bill, of being his (5)______. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 1.

In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each number. Ben committed (1)______ crime when he (2)______ a bank. Someone (3)______ the crime and told the police. The police charged him (4)______ the crime and put him behind bars. They also accused his twin brother, Bill, of being his (5)______. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 2.
questions.explanation: Ben committed a crime. So, c is the only option that is fitting into the situation

Ben committed (1)______ crime when he (2)______ a bank. Someone (3)______ the crime and told the police. The police charged him (4)______ the crime and put him behind bars. questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each number. Ben committed (1)______ crime when he (2)______ a bank. Someone (3)______ the crime and told the police. The police charged him (4)______ the crime and put him behind bars. They also accused his twin brother, Bill, of being his (5)______. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 5.
questions.explanation: Accomplice – a person who helped a criminal to commit the crime.Adventurer – interested in adventureColleague – co-workerAdmirer – a person who admires someone or something
